newsSince: Incremental edge-AI momentum: Accton/Gallopwave UAV edge-AI MoU (Jun 3), Astra SL2600 design wins ahead of plan, and the Astra SL2610 Coralboard launch with Google Research at Google I/O (Rosenblatt PT to $180). All slightly pre- or around June 9; nothing new since alters the weak HBM/packaging/CPO bottleneck relevance.

sources.label: Synaptics bullish targets as edge-AI deals build (StocksToTrade)

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node lens favors HBM/packaging/networking chokepoints; SYNA's edge-AI Astra/Coralboard wins are real but tangential to the node bottleneck. +79% YTD and accelerating PT raises ($155-180) signal crowding. No node-relevant change since June 9. Hold at 64.
verdicts.bull: Astra edge-AI design wins ahead of plan, Google Coralboard and UAV MoU build a credible edge-inference franchise ramping into 2027.
verdicts.neutral: Genuine edge-AI optionality but only selective AI exposure; less direct than HBM, advanced packaging or AI networking names.
verdicts.bear: +79% YTD and rising PTs imply crowding; core IoT/consumer cyclicality and no HBM/packaging chokepoint leave thin node-bottleneck alpha.
verdicts.primaryRisk: IoT/consumer demand cyclicality and valuation crowding

verdicts.rankRationale: On the co-packaged-optics lens SYNA has essentially no CPO exposure; the June edge-AI/UAV/Coralboard news is unrelated to optical interconnect. Prior remove call stands structurally; +79% YTD worsens asymmetry. Hold low at 111.
verdicts.bull: Edge-AI franchise momentum could broaden the IoT TAM and provide optionality adjacent to AI buildouts.
verdicts.neutral: Edge-AI optionality with low CPO exposure; not part of the co-packaged-optics bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: No meaningful CPO/optical link, +79% YTD and small $5.2B cap make it a poor fit for a scarce co-packaged-optics alpha basket.
